search._comment_connect_timeout: TCP connect timeout in seconds for academic search APIs (Crossref, PubMed, WoS, Elsevier, Springer).
search._comment_read_timeout: Response read timeout in seconds for academic search APIs.

fetch_strategy._comment_order: PDF/full-text retrieval order. `codex` is a disabled-by-default Codex Chrome extension handoff.

unpaywall._comment_enabled: When true, resolve DOI to Unpaywall OA locations before codex/browser acquisition. Unpaywall is not a download strategy and does not affect api or scihub.

codex_handoff._comment_download_watch_dir: Directory scanned only by ingest_codex_downloaded_pdf after a Codex Chrome Extension handoff. Default ~/Downloads follows Chrome's usual per-user download location and does not configure Chrome.
codex_handoff._comment_download_max_age_seconds: Maximum candidate PDF age after the handoff receipt is issued.
codex_handoff._comment_download_settle_seconds: Seconds a candidate PDF must remain size/mtime-stable before ingest.
codex_handoff._comment_download_settle_max_wait_second: Maximum seconds to wait for a candidate PDF to settle.
codex_handoff._comment_download_scan_recursive: When false, only scan the watch directory root. Recursive scanning is opt-in.

tdm._comment_order: Publisher API/TDM providers tried by the api fetch strategy.

sci_hub._comment_endpoints: Ordered Sci-Hub access endpoints. The first endpoint is tried first; later endpoints are fallbacks.
sci_hub._comment_fallback_mirror: Legacy compatibility endpoint used only when endpoints is omitted or empty.

headless_browser._comment_browser: Fallback system browser type when the managed browser is unavailable. Supported value: chrome.
headless_browser._comment_prefer_managed_browser: Prefer the GRaDOS-managed Chrome for Testing under GRADOS_HOME/browser/chromium.
headless_browser._comment_auto_install_managed_browser: Reserved bootstrap policy flag; run `grados setup` to install/update the managed browser.
headless_browser._comment_use_persistent_profile: Use GRADOS_HOME/browser/profile as a persistent browser profile to reduce repeated publisher checks.
headless_browser._comment_executable_path: Optional absolute browser executable override used after the managed browser when prefer_managed_browser=true.
headless_browser._comment_reuse_interactive_window: Reuse the same visible browser session while the process stays alive.
headless_browser._comment_keep_interactive_window_open: Keep the visible browser open after fetch attempts so completed verification can help later requests.
headless_browser._comment_close_pdf_page_after_capture: Close captured PDF tabs while keeping the reusable browser window/profile alive.
headless_browser._comment_deadline_seconds: Maximum wall-clock seconds for the browser main polling loop per DOI.
headless_browser._comment_networkidle_timeout: Ceiling in seconds for wait_for_load_state('networkidle'). See ADR-008.
headless_browser._comment_pdf_backfill_timeout: Timeout in seconds for browser context.request direct-PDF backfill; navigation deadlines are separate.
headless_browser._comment_poll_min_seconds: Starting sleep between browser main-loop iterations.
headless_browser._comment_poll_max_seconds: Upper bound for the browser main-loop sleep after exponential backoff.

parsing._comment_marker_timeout: Maximum time in milliseconds to wait for the isolated Marker subprocess.
parsing._comment_mineru_timeout: Maximum time in milliseconds to wait for MinerU's authenticated cloud parser.
parsing._comment_mineru_model_version: MinerU cloud model version. Use `vlm` for precise PDF parsing, or `pipeline` for the lighter model.
parsing._comment_mineru_language: MinerU language hint. Default `en` fits most academic PDFs; use `ch` for Chinese documents.

assets._comment_mode: Parser asset persistence mode. `all` saves all allowed parser assets, `referenced` keeps content-linked assets, and `none` disables asset bundles.
assets._comment_docling_image_scale: Image scale used by Docling when generating referenced page, picture, and table images.
assets._comment_max_asset_file_bytes: Maximum size in bytes for one persisted parser asset file.
assets._comment_max_asset_total_bytes: Maximum total size in bytes for one saved paper's asset bundle.
assets._comment_max_asset_inline_bytes: Maximum image size in bytes that read_paper_asset may return inline.
assets._comment_max_asset_count: Maximum number of parser assets saved for one paper.

security._comment_max_remote_pdf_bytes: Maximum remote PDF response size in bytes. The default is intentionally generous for normal papers.
security._comment_max_remote_text_bytes: Maximum remote native text, HTML, XML, or JSON article payload size in bytes.
security._comment_max_local_pdf_bytes: Maximum local PDF size in bytes for parse_pdf_file and import-pdfs.
security._comment_max_browser_capture_bytes: Maximum PDF body or download temp-file size captured by the browser strategy.
security._comment_max_mineru_zip_bytes: Maximum MinerU result zip size in bytes before markdown and asset extraction.
security._comment_max_mineru_full_md_bytes: Maximum uncompressed full.md size in bytes inside the MinerU result zip.

extract._comment_fetch_connect_timeout: TCP connect timeout in seconds for api / unpaywall / scihub HTTP calls.
extract._comment_fetch_read_timeout: Response read timeout in seconds for landing-page, HTML, XML, JSON, and non-PDF full-text fetches.
extract._comment_pdf_read_timeout: Response read timeout in seconds for direct remote PDF downloads. Size limits still enforce the maximum accepted PDF bytes.

indepth._comment_enabled: Default off. When enabled, search uses the same limit for metadata candidates and full-text attempts.
indepth._comment_auto_summarize: Generate query-independent paper_summary artifacts after successful full-text saves.

external_synthesis._comment_enabled: Default off. When true, GRaDOS may open its private ChatGPT Chrome profile only after verified evidence preparation; GRaDOS-validated Pro model and Pro Extended thinking route are protocol defaults recorded as metadata, not config keys.

research._comment_indepth: Indepth mode is disabled by default. Enable per request with `--indepth` or set research.indepth.enabled=true to make search materialize returned candidates.
research._comment_external_synthesis: Default-off GRaDOS-native ChatGPT Pro browser reviewer/synthesizer. GRaDOS sends only verified evidence packets, saves advisory output, and audits it.

indexing.query_instruction: Given a scientific literature search query, retrieve relevant abstracts and passages that answer the query
indexing._comment_provider: Default local embedding provider used for semantic indexing.
indexing._comment_model_id: Default embedding model. Harrier 270M is the stable local default; use 0.6B only when you have headroom.
indexing._comment_query_prompt_name: Harrier query-side prompt name. Query/document encoding is intentionally asymmetric.
indexing._comment_max_length: Recommended indexing length cap. Model max context is not the same as a safe local indexing length.
indexing._comment_batch_size: Embedding encode batch size. Use 0 for conservative auto-sizing by device.
indexing._comment_cache_dir: Optional model cache override. Leave empty to use GRaDOS_HOME/models/embedding.

retry_policy._comment_max_attempts: Total attempts including the first call. 1 disables retries.
retry_policy._comment_max_wait: Upper bound in seconds on a single backoff wait between attempts.
retry_policy._comment_respect_retry_after: Honor Retry-After / X-RateLimit-Reset response headers when true.

_comment_debug: Set to true to enable verbose benchmark logs.
_comment_academic_etiquette_email: Email for academic APIs (Crossref, Unpaywall). Change to your real institutional email.
_comment_api_keys: Plaintext API keys in config.json are a temporary import path only. GRaDOS will migrate them to the OS keychain on the next run and then clear them from this file.
_comment_indexing: Semantic indexing defaults. Changing model_id or section-aware chunking settings requires `grados reindex`.
_comment_retry_policy: Unified retry knobs for external HTTP calls (ADR-008). Retries cover 429, 5xx, network errors.
